title: ulmus procera f. marginata
intro: Ulmus procera f. marginata, also known as the Narrow-leaved Elm, is a variant of the Elm tree species (Ulmus procera) characterized by its narrower leaf margins. The bark and leaves of the Ulmus procera f. marginata plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These properties are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness the medicinal properties of the plant.
